message:
Last week we had a water main break two blocks over, and suddenly our neighborhood group chat went absolutely wild. 'Do we need to boil?' 'How much water should we have?' 'What do I even DO?' — and I'm sitting there realizing half my family has no idea what we actually have stashed at home.

So I texted a quick photo of our water supply and a simple list of what we keep together, and honestly? The relief in that chat was real. Not because we're some prepper family (we're really not), but because we'd thought about it *once* when things were calm.

I think that's the whole thing, right? You don't need to be intense about it. Just knowing where your important documents are, having some water on hand, maybe a bag that's actually packed — it takes the panic out of the equation when something unexpected happens.

Have you guys ever been surprised by how unprepared (or prepared!) you actually were in a moment?
